question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a spoofed ownerId that matches another account's ownerId, causing the repository to associate malicious hook storage updates with a victim's hook namespace, leading to unauthorized state mutation visible via findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a HOOKSTORE transaction that reuses an existing hookId belonging to another owner, and because findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn only filters by ownerId at query time, cause the importer to persist hook storage entries under the wrong owner, corrupting the hook state visible to legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered HOOKSTORE transaction with a hookId set to Long.MAX_VALUE or Long.MIN_VALUE, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to return unexpected results when lowerBound and upperBound are computed, leading to information disclosure of hooks belonging to other owners?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a submitted HOOKSTORE transaction to set hookId to 0, causing the importer to persist a hook with hookId=0 that falls within every findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween range query (lowerBound=0 by default), polluting all range-based hook lookups for the targeted ownerId?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a crafted storageUpdatesList that overwrites an existing hook's storage key-value pair, and because findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn returns the current state without version checking, cause consumers to read tampered hook storage values as if they were legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a HOOKSTORE transaction body to inject a hookId that is already registered under a victim's ownerId, causing the upsert logic in AbstractHook (UPSERTABLE_COLUMN_COALESCE) to silently overwrite the victim's adminKey with attacker-controlled bytes, leading to loss of hook administrative control?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction that sets the deleted flag to false on a previously deleted hook (by sending a non-empty value for a deleted storage slot), causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to resurface hooks that should no longer be active, leading to unauthorized hook re-activation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a HOOKSTORE transaction with a storageUpdatesList containing an empty value for a targeted key, causing HookStorage.setValue() to set deleted=true and DomainUtils.trim() to null the value, effectively deleting another account's hook storage entry via a tampered transaction, with the deletion confirmed by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn returning no results for that hookId?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple tampered HOOKSTORE transactions with sequential hookIds to flood the hook table for a victim's ownerId, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to return MAX_LIMIT results on every query and effectively denying legitimate hook lookups through resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a HOOKSTORE transaction to set the extensionPoint field to ACCOUNT_ALLOWANCE_HOOK on a hook they do not own, causing the hook to intercept allowance-related transactions for the victim account, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to expose this tampered hook configuration to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a tampered contractId pointing to a malicious EVM contract, causing the Hook entity persisted via the importer to reference an attacker-controlled contract, which is then returned by findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to callers who trust the contractId field for hook execution?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a HOOKSTORE transaction to set a hook's type to EVM (HookType.EVM) on a hook registered under a victim's ownerId, causing the hook execution engine to treat the hook as an EVM hook and execute attacker-controlled bytecode, with findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween confirming the tampered type is persisted?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a crafted hookId that collides with a hook in the allowExecHookIds execution queue (built by HookExecutionCollector.buildExecutionQueue()), causing the hook to be executed in the PreTx phase for HBAR/Token/NFT transfers it was not authorized for, with findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn confirming the hook's presence?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a HOOKSTORE transaction's storageUpdatesList to write a 32-byte key of all 0x00 bytes (MIN_KEY_BYTES as defined in HooksController), causing a collision with the minimum key boundary used in storage range queries, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return this entry in all inclusive range scans?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a HOOKSTORE transaction's storageUpdatesList to write a 32-byte key of all 0xFF bytes (MAX_KEY_BYTES as defined in HooksController), causing a collision with the maximum key boundary used in storage range queries, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return this entry in all inclusive range scans?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction that sets createdTimestamp to a value equal to lower(timestamp_range), causing the UPSERTABLE_COLUMN_COALESCE logic in AbstractHook to unconditionally overwrite existing fields (adminKey, contractId) rather than coalescing with existing values, effectively resetting a victim hook's configuration via a tampered transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction that sets the hook's deleted field to true via the upsert coalesce logic, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween to still return the hook (if the repository does not filter on deleted=false), allowing the attacker to observe that a hook exists even after it has been logically deleted?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a Collection<Long> hookIds parameter containing duplicate hookId values, causing findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return duplicate Hook entities in the result list, leading to double-processing of hook execution logic by downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn()] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with a HOOKSTORE transaction to set the ownerId to the numeric ID of a system account (e.g., account 0.0.800), causing the hook to be registered under a privileged system account's namespace, and findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn to return this hook when queried with the system account's ownerId?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a HOOKSTORE transaction with a hookId equal to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing an integer overflow when the HookServiceImpl computes upperBound = Math.min(upperBound, hookIdFilter.getInclusiveValue()), leading to findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween receiving a negative upperBound and returning an empty or incorrect result set?
